body_txt: This is an imperialist or colonialist man who has just come to take advantage of the natives with a friendly smile and a rifle on his shoulder. He only cares about winning a quick buck at everyone else's expense and exploiting the poor natives since he is convinced his race/culture is superior . Since he is either rich and powerful or in the middle of nowhere where nobody can hear your screams, he becomes the king of the place. He is likely to be Chased by Angry Natives led by a Badass Native either when declaring such dominion or somewhere else along the line. The character, usually a white one, was originally depicted as Western European (usually from Britain , France , Spain , or Germany , including Those Wacky Nazis ), but the United States and South Africa joined the group later. If this is the case, expect some Social Darwinist , White Man's Burden and A God Am I excuses for his actions (which usually consists of taking over a place, exploiting the natives and the land, selling them to his Human Traffickers , launching a Final Solution on the natives, and then leaving the place as an economic waste after the natives get fed up and kick him out). After all, who other than himself could tame the Lost World or Darkest Africa ? Some places you stand a good chance of seeing a non-white version of this trope are Korean (both north and south , but mostly north) and Chinese depictions of Imperial Japan . China may also be portrayed this way (whether Imperial China in media about their tributary states such as Korea and Vietnam , or the modern People's Republic in media about Tibet or East Turkestan ). Outside of Western Europe and the Americas, the trope goes for Russia in Central Asia , The Caucasus , Finland , and Eastern Europe (especially the Baltic states , Ukraine , and Poland ), and for Turkey in the Balkans, Caucasus (especially Armenia ), and Arab World (note that Arabs themselves may be depicted this way in non-Arab parts of Africa and Asia, including Kurdistan , though you're also reasonably likely to see Arab—or Turkish for that matter—empires depicted as relatively cosmopolitan and enlightened, if sometimes decadently orientalist , "Arabian Nights" Days settings). It's fairly common in works from Africa, India, Ireland, Latin America, and China since those are places where Imperialism hit hardest. It's also a stereotypical villain in Soviet works, and a good source for Western terrorism in those works. In more fantastical settings, there's often overlap between this trope and the Galactic Conquerer . The "conquistador" variety of Scary Dogmatic Aliens is often just this character but in space . Nazi Moon Base can be a result when the colonialists (including, but not necessarily, the Nazis) use Moon as their Space Base . An evil version of the Gentleman Adventurer , and evil variations of The Missionary qualify. The racial opposite is Mighty Whitey and the ideological one is Dirty Communists . Foil to the Noble Savage and Badass Native . Will likely be good friends with the Egomaniac Hunter (who may serve as The Dragon ) and Human Traffickers , if he isn't one himself in his spare time. Sub-Trope of Foreign Ruling Class , Politically Incorrect Villain , and No Historical Figures Were Harmed . Compare Those Wacky Nazis for similar ideologies, and Western Terrorists for the spiritual modern variations. "Great Replacement" Conspiracy Theory is a version of this where the posited colonialists are Villains With Good Publicity .
